reasoning: Re-opened WSMAIN.MAC:1421-1426. Gate is LDA GM.WAV / CMPA #4-1 (=3) / IFGE (block runs when GM.WAV>=3) / ANDA #1 / IFNE (GM.WAV odd) / JSR PMDAR. GM.WAV is 0-based: the sibling comment on line 1423 reads '?WAVE 4 OR HIGHER?' for GM.WAV>=3, so GM.WAV = human_wave - 1; line 1425 comment '?ANDIF EVEN (ODD-1) WAVE?' confirms GM.WAV odd = human EVEN. So ROM March set = GM.WAV in {3,5,7,...} = human {4,6,8,...}. Only one gameplay call site (WSMAIN:1426; the :1256 PMDAR is a music-table .WORD, not a call). Ours: sim.ts:890 predicate `wave>=3 && wave%2===1`. state.wave is 1-based (state.ts:528 doc, :673 wave:1). Verified the plumbing: the only wave-variable space call is sim.ts:767 `musicTrackFor('space', state.wave+1)` (the other two sites pass literal 1 / a non-space phase), so the predicate is evaluated on the UPCOMING 1-based wave → March fires on upcoming human {3,5,7,...}. ROM {4,6,8,...} and ours {3,5,7,...} are disjoint (one wave early, opposite parity). Divergence is real; recommended fix `wave>=4 && wave%2===0` is arithmetically coherent with the +1 plumbing. Held.

Re-opened WSMAIN.MAC:1919-1928. LDA GM.WAV / CMPA #4-1 / IFGE ('?WAVE 4 OR HIGHER?') / ANDA #1 / IFNE ('?ANDIF AN EVEN(ODD-1) WAVE?') / JSR SPKGRE / BRA 80$ / ENDIF / ENDIF / 80$ (empty). So SPKGRE (great-shot-kid) is spoken ONLY when GM.WAV>=3 && GM.WAV odd = human {4,6,8,...}; the else path falls straight through to the empty 80$ label (silent). Grepped all .MAC: SPKGRE has exactly ONE call site (WSMAIN:1924) — no unconditional path exists. Ours: sim.ts:755 pushes the greatShotKidThatWasOneInAMillion speech event UNCONDITIONALLY inside the `if (detonates)` block (its own comment even says 'cued on ANY port kill'), with no wave gate. So we say it on every port kill; ROM reserves it for the same even-wave-4+ set as the Imperial March. Divergence real; shares the GM.WAV base/parity root with U-005. Held.

Re-opened WSMAIN.MAC:1868-1892. Gate LDA BS.WAV / LSRA / IFCC selects BS.WAV even (Luke-trust-me branch); the ELSE (odd) branch is LDA PH.TIM / CMPA #16. / IFEQ / JSR SPKLET ('LET GO LUKE') / ELSE / CMPA #22. / IFEQ / JSR SPKSTR ('THE FORCE IS STRONG WITH THIS ONE'). So the ROM odd branch fires TWO lines: SPKLET@16 and SPKSTR@22. Grepped: SPKLET has a single call site (WSMAIN:1885). Ours: TRENCH_VOICE_CUES (sim.ts:909-917) odd branch contains only {timer:22,'theForceIsStrongInThisOne'} — letGoLuke is entirely absent from the table. The core claim (missing letGoLuke@16) is verified true regardless of parity mapping. Held.
corrections: The finding's reasoning ('Our even branch matching the ROM even branch byte-for-byte is strong evidence the wave-parity mapping is aligned') is wrong: the mapping is INVERTED, not aligned. BS.WAV = GM.WAV clamped (WSMAIN:1702-1707) and is 0-based (line 1870 comment 'BEGIN WITH WAVE 0'), while our parity comes from 1-based state.wave (sim.ts:610). So ROM's BS.WAV-odd branch fires on human waves {2,4,6,...} but our parity:'odd' fires on {1,3,5,...}. Adding {timer:16,parity:'odd','letGoLuke'} is still correct RELATIVE to our existing (also-'odd') theForceIsStrongInThisOne entry, but note that the whole trench-cue table is wired to the inverted wave set (same base-offset bug flagged under U-008), so it will play on the wrong waves until the BS.WAV/wave parity is reconciled (sw3-7).

reasoning: Re-opened WSMAIN.MAC:1871-1879. Even branch (BS.WAV even via LSRA/IFCC): CMPA #16. / JSR SPKTRU ('LUKE, TRUST ME'); CMPA #24. / JSR SPKYAU ('YAHOO, YOURE ALL CLEAR KID'). Thresholds #16. #24. carry trailing dots = decimal in the radix-16 file, so 16/24 are integer frame counts. Ours: sim.ts:914-915 {timer:16,'lukeTrustMe'} and {timer:24,'youreAllClearKid'}. The narrow data claim — same two (line, frame-threshold) pairs in our table and the ROM even branch — is genuinely true and verified. Held as a DATA match only; see corrections for why the title 'cues match' overstates fidelity.
corrections: Two defects the 'match' framing hides. (1) TIMING (ties to T-008): the trenchTimer that fires these advances `state.trenchTimer + 1` once per stepGame call (sim.ts:609), and stepGame runs on the shared @arcade/shared createLoop whose hz DEFAULTS to 60 (main.ts:136 passes no hz; loop.js:39 hz=60). ROM PH.TIM increments once per 20.508 Hz game frame. So our cues fire at 16/60=0.27 s and 24/60=0.40 s vs ROM 16/20.508=0.78 s and 24/20.508=1.17 s — 2.93x early, bunched into the first ~0.4 s. Frame-count values are right; wall-clock timing is not. (2) PARITY-BASE INVERSION: the reasoning claim 'the gate ... (carry clear = BS.WAV even) corresponds to our parity:even' is FALSE. BS.WAV = GM.WAV (0-based, WSMAIN:1702-1707), our parity uses 1-based state.wave (sim.ts:610), so BS.WAV even <=> human {1,3,5,...} while parity:'even' <=> human {2,4,6,...}. Our even cues are wired to the ROM's odd-wave set. The lines/thresholds match the ROM even branch as data, but ride the wrong waves at the wrong wall-clock time — it is not the clean CONFIRMED the title implies; annotate as 'values correct; parity base inverted and timing 2.93x fast (blocked on T-008 / sw3-7).'

reasoning: Re-opened WSXPLD.MAC:156-237 (BGAXP). Three NXTFRE allocations: TP$TI1 'TYPE 1, TIE FIGHTER' with LEFT-wing offsets (M$AY/M$BY/M$CY negated, lines 162-189), TP$TI2 'TYPE 2, TIE FIGHTER RIGHT WING' (lines 193-217), TP$TI3 'TYPE 3, TIE FIGHTER CENTER GLOBE' (lines 221-237). So the headline — exactly three explosion-queue pieces with a left/right lateral wing split — is verified true. Ours: render.ts:394-396 draws exactly three fragment models, FRAG_1 at (-s,0,0), FRAG_2 at (+s,0,0): the left/right lateral split matches. Headline holds.
corrections: STRIKE the false parenthetical: 'The third piece differs in identity (ROM: center globe/cockpit ball; ours: a third wing fragment on +z).' It does NOT differ. Verified models.ts:234-239: TIE_WING_FRAG_3 has name 'TIE Fragment Cabin', vertices `TIE_FIGHTER.vertices.slice(-28)` (the aft cabin/globe), i.e. the ROM's TP$TI3 CENTER GLOBE. The symbol name 'WING_FRAG_3' is a misnomer; the geometry is the globe, so the third-piece identity actually MATCHES the ROM (consistent with pair-models M-002, not in conflict with it). Only its render placement (+z drift, render.ts:396) is a render-only tell. The false 'differs in identity' clause must not ship as proof-of-divergence.

reasoning: Re-opened WSXPLD.MAC. Per-piece life timers loaded into XP$TMR: the two wings LDA #18 (lines 165, 196), the center globe LDA #10 (line 224). Radix is 16 (WSCOMN default; immediates carry no trailing period), so #18 = 0x18 = 24 decimal frames and #10 = 0x10 = 16 decimal frames — decoded correctly. DOXPLD (WSXPLD:485-490) does LDA XP$TMR / IFNE / DEC XP$TMR ('COUNT DOWN TO ZERO'), decrementing by 1 per call; grepped its 7 JSR sites (WSMAIN:1402,1469,1525,1651,1743,1775,2053) — each is in a distinct per-phase handler and only one phase runs per frame, so DOXPLD executes exactly once per 20.508 Hz game frame. Thus wings = 24/20.508 = 1.170 s, globe = 16/20.508 = 0.780 s. Ours: state.ts:247 TIE_DEATH_SECONDS = 0.7 for all three (render.ts:390 computes one age fraction `f` and applies it to every fragment; the sim drops all pieces at that single constant). So our wings vanish at 0.7 s vs ROM 1.17 s (0.7/1.17 = 0.60, ~40% too short) and we give the wings no longer life than the globe. Numbers, radix, and rate all check out. Held.
